subject: Wire cross-page navigation and shared layout
description:
Make the three pages feel like one product.

Walk the repo first: read CLAUDE.md for current component structure and routing setup.

Work:
- Shared sticky header with nav links (Campaign, Community, Profile) - consistent across all pages
- Active state on current nav link
- Organizer name on campaign page links to /profile/:id
- Leaderboard entries on community page link to /profile/:id
- Trending campaigns on community page link to /campaign/:id
- Profile campaign history cards link to /campaign/:id
- Verify data consistency: navigate between pages and confirm same organizer shows same name/stats/trust score everywhere
- Consider: breadcrumb or page indicator (the spec mentions "breadcrumb context" but with peer pages, a simple page indicator may work better)

This is a critical quality gate. The spec says "seamlessly integrated and intuitive" - this task is where that's proven.

Write back to CLAUDE.md:
- Navigation patterns established
- Any shared layout components (header, footer, page wrapper)
- Cross-page consistency verification notes

changes: Created app/src/components/SiteHeader.jsx - sticky header with blur backdrop, logo, nav pills with active state
Updated App.jsx - added SiteHeader, fixed default route from /campaign/1 to /campaign/campaign-1
Updated CampaignPage.jsx - sticky donate panel offset from top-8 to top-20 to clear header
learnings: Header height h-14 (56px) means sticky elements below need top-20 (80px) offset for comfortable spacing
Nav pills use pathname.startsWith for matching so /campaign/campaign-2 still highlights the Campaign pill
